functions.name: nixos_compose.utils.copy_tree
documentation: Copy all files in the source path to the destination path.

methods.name: nixos_compose.context.Context.wlog
documentation: Logs a warning message to stderr.

methods.name: nixos_compose.context.Context.elog
documentation: Logs a error message to stderr.

methods.name: nixos_compose.context.Context.vlog
documentation: Logs a message to stderr only if verbose is enabled.

classes.name: nixos_compose.context.DeprecatedCmdDecorator
documentation:
This is a decorator which can be used to mark cmd as deprecated. It will
result in a warning being emmitted when the command is invoked.

functions.name: nixos_compose.driver.generate_driver_symbols
documentation:
This generates a file with symbols of the test-driver code that can be used
in user's test scripts. That list is then used by pyflakes to lint those
scripts.

classes.name: nixos_compose.driver.driver.Driver

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.driver.Driver.subtest
documentation: Group logs under a given test name

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.driver.Driver.run_tests
documentation: Run the test script (for non-interactive test runs)

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.driver.Driver.join_all
documentation: Wait for all machines to shut down

A handle to the driver that sets up the environment
and runs the tests

classes.name: nixos_compose.driver.vlan.VLan
documentation:
This class handles a VLAN that the run-vm scripts identify via its
number handles. The network's lifetime equals the object's lifetime.

functions.name: nixos_compose.actions.realpath_from_store
documentation: Retrieve from path from different store location if needed

functions.name: nixos_compose.actions.get_ip_ssh_port
documentation: Retrieve host's ip address and ssh port from deployment info

functions.name: nixos_compose.actions.kill_proc_tree
documentation:
Kill a process tree (including grandchildren) with signal
"sig" and return a (gone, still_alive) tuple.
"on_terminate", if specified, is a callback function which is
called as soon as a child terminates.
From: https://psutil.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#kill-process-tree

functions.name: nixos_compose.nxc_execo.get_oar_job_nodes_nxc
documentation:
Brother of the "get_oar_job_nodes" function from execo
but does the mapping with roles from NXC

functions.name: nixos_compose.tools.kataract.elog
documentation: B  = cmd_base.substitute({'inner_cmd' : 'until nc -z localhost 5556; do sleep 0.01; done; nc -l 4444 | tee >(cat > /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/5555) | base64 -d > /tmp/yopB & nc -l 4446'})
A = 'until nc -z localhost 4446; do sleep 0.01; done; base64 /tmp/vm-state-client1/client1.qcow2  >/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/4444'

classes.name: nixos_compose.flavours.vm.VmBasedFlavour
documentation: The Vm Ramdisk flavour. This is flavour provides a system image to be executed with QEMU and use memory only for root system. By consequence lot of ram is used around 2Go minimum by node.

classes.name: nixos_compose.flavours.ClassNotFound
documentation: Raised if one of the lookup functions didn't find a matching class.

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_clean.cli
documentation: Clean the nxc folder and nxc.json file

functions.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.retry
documentation:
Call the given function repeatedly, with 1 second intervals,
until it returns True or a timeout is reached.

classes.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.VmStartCommand
documentation:
The Base Start Command knows how to append the necesary
runtime qemu options as determined by a particular test driver
run. Any such start command is expected to happily receive and
append additional qemu args.

classes.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.NixStartScript
documentation:
A start script from nixos/modules/virtualiation/qemu-vm.nix
that also satisfies the requirement of the BaseStartCommand.
These Nix commands have the particular charactersitic that the
machine name can be extracted out of them via a regex match.
(Admittedly a _very_ implicit contract, evtl. TODO fix)

classes.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_for_unit
documentation:
Wait for a systemd unit to get into "active" state.
Throws exceptions on "failed" and "inactive" states as well as
after timing out.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.shell_interact
documentation:
Allows you to interact with the guest shell
Should only be used during test development, not in the production test.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.succeed
documentation: Execute each command and check that it succeeds.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.fail
documentation: Execute each command and check that it fails.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_until_succeeds
documentation:
Wait until a command returns success and return its output.
Throws an exception on timeout.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_until_fails
documentation:
Wait until a command returns failure.
Throws an exception on timeout.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_until_tty_matches
documentation:
Wait until the visible output on the chosen TTY matches regular
expression. Throws an exception on timeout.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_for_file
documentation: Waits until the file exists in machine's file system.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.copy_from_host_via_shell
documentation:
Copy a file from the host into the guest by piping it over the
shell into the destination file. Works without host-guest shared folder.
Prefer copy_from_host for whenever possible.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.copy_from_host
documentation:
Copy a file from the host into the guest via the `shared_dir` shared
among all the VMs (using a temporary directory).

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.copy_from_vm
documentation:
Copy a file from the VM (specified by an in-VM source path) to a path
relative to `$out`. The file is copied via the `shared_dir` shared among
all the VMs (using a temporary directory).

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.dump_tty_contents
documentation: Debugging: Dump the contents of the TTY<n>

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine._start_vm
documentation: Should work for vm-ramdisk (tested) and other qemu based (not tested)

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.wait_for_x
documentation:
Wait until it is possible to connect to the X server.  Note that
testing the existence of /tmp/.X11-unix/X0 is insufficient.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.forward_port
documentation:
Forward a TCP port on the host to a TCP port on the guest.
Useful during interactive testing.

methods.name: nixos_compose.driver.machine.Machine.block
documentation:
Make the machine unreachable by shutting down eth1 (the multicast
interface used to talk to the other VMs).  We keep eth0 up so that
the test driver can continue to talk to the machine.

A handle to the machine with this name, that also knows how to manage
the machine lifecycle with the help of a start script / command.

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_connect.cli
documentation:
Opens one or more terminal sessions into the deployed nodes. By default, it will connect to all nodes, but we can specify which ones to connect to.

To connect to several machines at once `nxc` use a tmux (terminal multiplexer) session. Feel free to refer to the [tmux documentation](https://github.com/tmux/tmux/wiki) (or its [cheatsheet](https://tmuxcheatsheet.com/)), especially for the shortcuts to navigate between the different tabs.

## Examples

- `nxc connect`

    Open a Tmux session with one panel for each node.

- `nxc connect server`

    Connect to the `server` node. It runs on the current shell (Tmux is not used in this case)

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_build.cli
documentation:
Builds the composition.

It generates a `build` folder which stores symlinks to the closure associated to a composition. The file name of the symlink follows this structure  `[composition-name]::[flavour]`

## Examples

- `nxc build -f vm`

    Build the `vm` flavour of your composition.

- `nxc build -C oar::g5k-nfs-store`

    Build the `oar` composition with the `g5k-nfs-store` flavour.

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_build.get_flavours
documentation: Returns the json representation of the available flavours

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_driver.cli
documentation:
Run the driver to execute the given script to interact with the deployed environment.
The script is a python script similar to nixos-test script. See the [NixOS manual on nixos-tests](https://nixos.org/manual/nixos/unstable/#sec-writing-nixos-tests) for more details.

```admonish warning
Be aware that unlike Nixos-test that only support virtual machines, `nxc` supports many flavours and VM specific features are not supported.
```

## Examples

- `nxc driver -t`

   Run the script defined in the composition

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_helper.cli
documentation:
Specific and contextual helper information (e.g. g5k_script path for Grid'5000)
Warning: Experimental command, may be removed in the future or change without backward compatibility care.

functions.name: examples.execo.execo_script.throw_on_problem
documentation: Raise if the given process did not finish properly (meaning attribute finished_ok is False)

functions.name: examples.execo.execo_script.reserve_nodes
documentation:
---
param walltime: the duration of the job, in seconds (or a datetime, or a string as
  expected by the oarsub program)
---

classes.name: examples.execo.execo_script.FailedProcessError
documentation: Exception raised when an Execo process failed (meaning its attribute finished_ok is False)

functions.name: nixos_compose.commands.cmd_start.cli
documentation:
Starts a set of nodes using the previous build.

`ROLE_DISTRIBUTION_FILE` is and optional YAML file describing how many instances of each role are expected.

## Examples

- `nxc start`

   Start the last built composition.

- `nxc start role-distrib.yaml`

    With the file `role-distrib.yaml` written as this:

    ```yaml
    nfsServerNode: 1
    nfsClientNode: 2
    ```

    Instantiates two nodes with the role `nfsClientNode` and one only with the role `nfsServerNode`. Of course, these roles have to be described beforehand in a `composition.nix` file.
